is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum isolates from Lambaréné, Gabon; predominant species is P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfmdr1 codon 86 (N86Y) and Pfcrt K76T mutation; both are curated target positions (Pfmdr1:86, Pfcrt:76).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: 2002 study: 86Y prevalence 89% (30/34 exclusive plus 3 mixed), N86 11%; codon 184 not target. pfcrt K76T: 'all but one' of 34 carried K76T (33/34).

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples from Lambaréné, Gabon — a named town (sub-national location) at the Albert Schweitzer Hospital.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: 1995 samples from chemotherapy study Jan 1995–Jan 1996; 2002 in vitro surveillance study Feb–June 2002. Each collection window is within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical/field isolates collected in Lambaréné, Gabon (African country); lab strains 3D7, HB3, 7G8, Dd2 used only as controls.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical genotyping study of field isolates.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples derived from previously published studies: 1995 chemotherapy trial (refs 13,14) and 2002 in vitro surveillance study (ref 11). Mawili-Mboumba 2002 (ref 14) also reported pfmdr1 alleles from the same 1995 Gabonese samples, suggesting possible overlap.

supplement.evidence: Marker frequencies for pfmdr1 codon 86 and pfcrt K76T (from the 2002 subset) are given in the main text; no supplementary data pointer needed for extractable frequencies.

assessment.summary: Study of pfmdr1 amplification and point mutations in P. falciparum isolates from Lambaréné, Gabon, collected in 1995 (chemotherapy trial) and 2002 (in vitro surveillance). The 2002 subset (n=34 genotyped) reports extractable frequencies for pfmdr1 N86Y (86Y prevalence 89%, N86 11%) and pfcrt K76T (all but one of 34 = 33/34 carried K76T). Location is Lambaréné (sub-national) and collection windows are within 3 years, satisfying all criteria. Duplicate risk is moderate because samples derive from prior published studies of the same isolates.
